design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse levels using acrobatic platforming and sword combat; slice through enemies and environmental obstacles to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigating hostile environments with precise timing and combat positioning while managing limited mobility options
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a nimble cyborg ninja executing stylish aerial sword combat across exotic locations
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strider prioritizes fluid, responsive movement and sword-based combat over collectibles or character upgrades; progression is entirely skill-based with no power-ups to modify playstyle

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with game over upon depletion; continues available
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present; allows resuming from death points
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoint system; progress saved at level transitions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No difficulty selection; fixed challenge level. Players must master movement mechanics and timing to overcome obstacles

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression; each stage must be completed to access the next
replayability_hooks: High skill ceiling encourages speedrunning and no-death runs
Multiple enemy placements and platforming patterns reward mastery
Limited lives create tension for perfectionists seeking clean runs

world_building_techniques: Exotic level themes (airports, mountains, underwater facilities) suggest international espionage setting
Visual design communicates environment hazards and story context without exposition

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based combat encounters requiring memorization of attack sequences and precise timing to exploit vulnerabilities

environmental_storytelling: Industrial/technological facility aesthetics suggest hostile corporate or military installation environments
Enemy types and obstacle design communicate level themes
